# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController  
    def process_image    
        @filename = params[:file].tempfile.path # get image temp file path        
     	format =  params['img']['input'] ? "#[0]" : ""      	# is input format specified? if so, use that instead of default else keep as blank  		 			   					          				    	 
        size = "15%x15%" # set a fixed width and height for the output image. user can provide different values based on requirements	            	   								     	       	     	        							      						                 if request params include :w or: h, then use them as is otherwise keep blank 		
        command = "convert -resize %s% \"% s\" '% [0]' '\'-quality 95%%'" %[format, size , @filename ] # format the conversion using imagemagick convert. See above comment for an explanation of what each part means  	          			      
        system(command) ? (render plain: "Conversion Complete") : ( render plain:"Failed to Convert Image" )         					    		 												         end                						              if request[:file].present?     							    unless command.match(/convert/) # only run when there is a file and image convert wasn't already called  			          
      	end       	 	   	if @filename                              	      ? (render plain: "Image File has not been found") :( render plain:"Failed to find Image" )									     if command.match(/failed/i) # only run when conversion failed 		          end        							    unless request[:file].present?  	end
